meaning: n.[希神]忒伊亚(Hyperion之妻,太阳神Helios之母)
examples: The sun god, son of Hyperion and Theia, depicted as driving his chariot across the sky from east to west daily.
It is believed that the moon formed when the nascent Earth and Theia collided, sending materials out into space.
The resulting impact blasted the outer layers of Theia and Earth into orbit, which eventually coalesced under their own gravity to form the moon.
